signals.reasoning: This is a position-management decision first. The live BLUR long has fallen to about $2.45 unrealized from a $6.71 peak, retaining only 37% of peak P&L, and the request marks it urgent. Even though the mechanical board is still bullish, the trade has already given back too much. I would close and protect realized gains now.

BTC remains liquid and stable, but the board still labels it no-trade and the directional edge is weak. Slightly negative funding offers modest support, yet macro remains RED so there is not enough asymmetry for a new position.

ETH is still a cleaner fade than BTC: slightly negative price trend, now positive funding, and a bearish mechanical lean. It is not an aggressive short, but directionally it still favors downside over upside.
SOL is mixed. Funding has turned mildly positive, but the broader structure is still not strong enough to justify either an aggressive short or a new long in a RED macro environment.

TNSR remains one of the best fresh longs on the sheet. It still has explosive 24h momentum, deeply negative funding, and elite squeeze scores. Confidence is limited only by wide spread and the still-risk-off macro backdrop.
TON has mild positive price action, but funding is positive and the mechanical edge is only neutral. It is tradable, just not compelling enough to outrank the better long candidates.
MAV has a small upward bias, but liquidity and spread quality are poor and funding is not supportive of a squeeze. Too noisy for a high-quality entry.

DYM still screens as a reduced-size continuation long with positive price expansion, negative funding, and strong crowding/squeeze scores. The main reason confidence is only moderate is the wide spread and thin market quality.

GRASS remains a mild bearish fade: slightly negative price trend, positive funding, and a bearish mechanical lean. It is not the strongest short on the board, but directionally downside still has the edge.
CHILLGUY is still up on the day, but the spread is extremely poor and funding is positive, so this is not a clean squeeze continuation. Better to leave it on the watchlist than chase it.
GRIFFAIN has solid momentum, but funding is positive and execution quality is still mediocre. It is interesting, but not a cleaner long than TNSR or DYM.
